claude.output_text:
```json
{"findings":[
{"finding_id":"F-001","severity":"critical","category":"test-gap","title":"PreCompact-hook reliability is an unvalidated gating assumption, yet 1.12a DoD depends on it","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":282,"symbol":"1.12a DoD bullet 1 + Open Question 1","snippet":"hooks/precompact.sh writes a derived checkpoint artifact ... on PreCompact event ... (Open Q1) Does the PreCompact hook fire reliably in headless Claude Code (orchestrator subagent runs)? Needs a 10-minute spike before 1.12a"},"recommendation":"Make the 10-minute spike a hard precondition with a named artifact (e.g. hooks/_spike/precompact-fires.log plus a pass/fail record in a bead) and block 1.12a behind it. If the hook does not fire in orchestrator subagent/Task contexts, rework DoD to place checkpoint writes in end-of-unit traps and Stop hooks only; do not ship a checkpoint writer that silently no-ops under the most common exhaustion scenario.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12a-precompact-unvalidated"},

{"finding_id":"F-002","severity":"high","category":"maintainability","title":"Resume identity scheme is under-specified: run_id is per-swarm-run invocation, not per-pipeline","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":250,"symbol":"1.12-N4","snippet":"operators can recover from a telemetry row by first resolving `run_id -> bd_epic_id`"},"recommendation":"runs.schema.json#v1 defines `issue_id` (swarm-do/schemas/telemetry/runs.schema.json:117) and the README states 'one row per swarm-run invocation' — a full pipeline emits many run_ids. Specify: (a) which run_id you resolve from (last? first? any?), (b) a new explicit field `bd_epic_id` in runs.v2 or the companion event ledger, and (c) the exact lookup (telemetry scan vs bd show metadata). Without this, `swarm resume <bd-id>` cannot be round-tripped from a telemetry row.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12-N4-runid-to-epic-mapping"},

{"finding_id":"F-003","severity":"high","category":"maintainability","title":"runs.v2 vs companion run_events.v1.jsonl is left as an either/or — a frozen-schema decision that blocks implementation","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":294,"symbol":"1.12a DoD telemetry bullet","snippet":"do not mutate frozen runs.v1 in place. Ship either runs.v2 or a companion run_events.v1.jsonl carrying checkpoint_written, handoff_triggered, and handoff_reason events, then update reports/TUI to join it."},"recommendation":"Pick one before executing 1.12a. runs.v1 is declared frozen at swarm-do/schemas/telemetry/runs.schema.json:5 and consumers already treat it as stable. Recommend `run_events.v1.jsonl` (no schema rev, additive, joinable on run_id) because mutating to runs.v2 forces TUI, swarm-validate, and every existing reader to version-branch. Nail down event schema keys, required/optional fields, and the join rule in the plan — otherwise writer work cannot start.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12a-runs-v2-vs-events-indecision"},

{"finding_id":"F-004","severity":"high","category":"maintainability","title":"BEADS-vs-checkpoint state authority is asserted but the mirror contract and drift algorithm are absent","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":285,"symbol":"1.12a DoD bullet 1 + Risks/non-goals","snippet":"Resume-critical state is mirrored to BEADS child issues / .beads/context/execution-state.md; checkpoint drift is detectable. ... BEADS remains the authoritative resume state; telemetry JSONL and runs/<run_id>/checkpoint.v1.json are derived artifacts"},"recommendation":"'Drift is detectable' is not a contract. Define: (a) exact mirror payload written to BEADS (JSON shape, location — notes vs design vs a new child), (b) what fields MUST match between checkpoint.v1.json and BEADS for non-drift, (c) the detection algorithm (diff keys, hash compare), and (d) the exit path when drift is found. Without this, 1.12a has no objective test for 'resume cleanly', and BEADS-as-SoT is aspirational.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12a-state-authority-contract-missing"},

{"finding_id":"F-005","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"Writer token/tool-call watchdog has no viable observation channel into a running Claude Agent()","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":184,"symbol":"1.12-N1","snippet":"The watchdog counts observable tool-result events, elapsed wall time, and captured output bytes, and uses token estimates only when real token fields are unavailable."},"recommendation":"runs.schema.json (lines 178-182) declares tool_call_count 'not observable from swarm-run in Phase 9a' and Claude Agent() invoked from swarm-do/skills/swarm-do/SKILL.md:55 returns a single final message — there is no intra-run tool-result stream to the orchestrator. Name the actual source: PostToolUse hook events joined on run_id, a Claude Code session log path, or output-byte tailing of a named artifact. Otherwise the watchdog is unimplementable and the 'tool_calls=50' default (DoD bullet 3) cannot be enforced.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"1.12-N1-watchdog-no-observation-channel"},

{"finding_id":"F-006","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"HANDOFF_REQUESTED marker has no defined channel or schema","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":189,"symbol":"1.12-N1","snippet":"if the writer notices it is near budget, it stops, writes a progress note to its bead, and emits HANDOFF_REQUESTED."},"recommendation":"Specify: where the marker appears (bead note text sentinel, writer_status enum value, stderr line, JSON field in final message), the exact string/shape, and the orchestrator parse rule. runs.schema.json:196-200 enumerates writer_status as DONE|DONE_WITH_CONCERNS|BLOCKED|NEEDS_CONTEXT — HANDOFF_REQUESTED is not there, so either extend the enum (schema change) or define an out-of-band channel. Without this, the 'cooperative fallback' cannot be tested and the max-2-handoff cap cannot be counted.","confidence":0.95,"fingerprint":"1.12-N1-handoff-marker-undefined"},

{"finding_id":"F-007","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"Observer-as-hook (N3) cannot observe swarm-run Codex subprocesses or worktree writer subagents","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":230,"symbol":"1.12-N3","snippet":"Replace the second-session observer with a PostToolUse / Stop hook (shell script) that writes a structured observation row to telemetry/observations.jsonl."},"recommendation":"Claude Code PostToolUse/Stop hooks fire in the harness session, not inside spawned Agent() subagents or swarm-run's Codex subprocess (bin/swarm-run:70-72). The insights cited in 'Origin signal' are writer-subagent events. Clarify scope: (a) what hook actually captures, (b) a separate mechanism for non-Claude/Codex backends (e.g. swarm-run emits observation rows itself from the EXIT trap), and (c) schema fields that only make sense at each layer. Otherwise observations.jsonl will be silent for exactly the failures that motivated it.","confidence":0.8,"fingerprint":"1.12-N3-hook-scope-mismatch"},

{"finding_id":"F-008","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"Checkpoint artifact path is asserted without matching the existing telemetry layout","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":284,"symbol":"1.12a DoD bullet 1","snippet":"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/runs/<run_id>/checkpoint.v1.json"},"recommendation":"Existing artifacts live under ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/telemetry/*.jsonl (swarm-do/schemas/telemetry/README.md:26, 139-144) and per-run provider artifacts are written to a 'swarm run artifact directory' (README.md:99-103) whose path contract is not defined in the plan. Either (a) align the checkpoint path to an already-contracted run-artifact directory and reference it, or (b) ship the run-artifact directory contract in 1.12a as an explicit sub-item. The current path is invented and will collide with the future run-artifact layout.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12a-checkpoint-path-layout-drift"},

{"finding_id":"F-009","severity":"high","category":"security","title":"Auto-merge default for swarm resume on drifted state is an unsafe default after a crash","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":389,"symbol":"Open Question 2","snippet":"Should swarm resume <bd-id> merge completed units automatically ... Default to auto-merge with --dry-run to opt-out."},"recommendation":"Resume runs are by definition post-crash — drift between BEADS child state, worktree commits, and the integration branch is most likely precisely when you would want a human in the loop. Invert the default: no-merge by default, explicit --merge flag to opt in. Tie the opt-in to a spec-review APPROVED verdict on every unit being merged. Auto-merging silently consumes worktree work the operator never reviewed.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"1.12a-resume-automerge-default-unsafe"},

{"finding_id":"F-010","severity":"medium","category":"test-gap","title":"Smallest-useful-spike deliverable is named as a precondition but its acceptance is not defined","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":386,"symbol":"Open Question 1","snippet":"Needs a 10-minute spike before 1.12a; source prior art proves hook registration shape, not headless reliability."},"recommendation":"Add a 'Spike' sub-section to 1.12a with: (a) fixture (minimal Agent() invocation that triggers compaction), (b) pass criterion (PreCompact event fires and the shell script runs with non-empty stdin context), (c) artifact location for the recorded outcome, (d) decision tree for each outcome (fires→build writer; does-not-fire→rework DoD around Stop + end-of-unit trap). Without this, 'smallest useful spike' is rhetorical.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12a-spike-acceptance-undefined"},

{"finding_id":"F-011","severity":"medium","category":"bug","title":"Hook env contract (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA availability to hook scripts) is assumed, not specified","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":284,"symbol":"1.12a DoD bullet 1","snippet":"hooks/precompact.sh writes ... at ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/runs/<run_id>/checkpoint.v1.json"},"recommendation":"Metaswarm's SessionStart hook receives structured context via stdin JSON — not env vars. Specify whether hooks/precompact.sh gets CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA from inherited env or must parse it from stdin, and where run_id comes from (hooks fire once per session, not once per run_id). Otherwise the script will fail at runtime on the very event it was built to handle.","confidence":0.75,"fingerprint":"1.12a-hook-env-contract"},

{"finding_id":"F-012","severity":"medium","category":"bug","title":"Watchdog breach has no termination mechanism for an in-flight Claude Agent()","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":190,"symbol":"1.12-N1","snippet":"Orchestrator triggers handoff on either watchdog breach or writer marker, spawns a fresh writer with the progress note + minimal delta"},"recommendation":"Claude Agent() invoked from the orchestrator skill (SKILL.md:55, 86) returns on its own — the orchestrator has no kill handle mid-flight. Spell out: do we (a) wait for the writer to drain and discard its result, (b) rely entirely on the cooperative HANDOFF_REQUESTED path for claude-backed writers, (c) use SIGTERM only for the swarm-run subprocess path? The current plan reads as if (a)+(c) and treats the handoff as immediate — it is not.","confidence":0.8,"fingerprint":"1.12-N1-watchdog-no-kill-handle"},

{"finding_id":"F-013","severity":"medium","category":"maintainability","title":"Two handoffs per unit cap is defined but retry accounting is not crash-durable","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":193,"symbol":"1.12-N1","snippet":"Max 2 handoffs per unit (third is a hard fail, escalated as SPEC_TOO_LARGE)."},"recommendation":"Handoff counts must survive context-overflow/compaction (the very trigger N1 fires on). Specify the durable store — a BEADS child-issue counter, a field in the work-unit artifact, or a run_events row — and the read path on resume. Otherwise a resumed run can infinitely handoff, which is the opposite of the budget cap's intent.","confidence":0.8,"fingerprint":"1.12-N1-handoff-count-durability"},

{"finding_id":"F-014","severity":"low","category":"maintainability","title":"observations.jsonl schema listed inline but not registered in telemetry ledger table","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":231,"symbol":"1.12-N3","snippet":"Schema: ts, run_id, phase_id, event_type, tool, file_paths, bead_ids, diff_size_bytes."},"recommendation":"swarm-do/schemas/telemetry/README.md (lines 12-20) contains a generated ledger table registered via swarm_do.telemetry.gen. Add observations.v1 to the LEDGERS registry in the plan DoD (schema file path, filename, fallback count) so 1.12a ships a registered ledger, not an orphan JSONL.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"1.12-N3-observations-not-registered"}
]}
```
